title: Parenthood Tax Rebate (PTR)
text:
Parenthood Tax Rebate (PTR) - SupportGoWhere
Parenthood Tax Rebate (PTR)
Share link
Provides a one-off tax rebate of up to $20,000 per child to eligible parents to encourage them to have more children.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Tax rebate of up to $20,000 per child for eligible parents
Parenthood Tax Rebate (PTR) may be shared between parents based on an agreed apportionment
Unutilised balance can be carried forward to offset future tax payable and / or transferred between spouses
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Rebate amount increases for each child, with $5,000 for the first, $10,000 for the second and $20,000 for all subsequent children

Parents may share the PTR based on an agreed apportionment

Unutilised PTR will be carried forward to offset future income tax payable

It may also be transferred online via myTaxPortal between spouses

Who is this for?

Singapore tax residents who are married, divorced or widowed in the relevant year

Different qualifying conditions for your child will apply based on the following:

Children born or adopted on or after 1 Jan 2008

Children born or adopted before 1 Jan 2008

Please note that PTR is not available for your first child as well as from your fifth child onwards

Child is a Singapore Citizen at the time of birth/marriage/legal adoption or within 12 months thereafter

Additional conditions determining your eligibility are as follows:

Children given for adoption

If married is dissolved under a court order

If spouse passes away

For more information on the qualifying conditions, please visit IRAS’ PTR page

Applicants may use the PTR Eligibility Tool to check their eligibility

How to apply?

For e-Filing:

If this is your first time claiming this relief:

Log in with Singpass or Singpass Foreign user Account (SFA) at myTax Portal

Navigate to “Individuals” > “File Income Tax Return”

Select “File” to File Income Tax Return

Go to “4. Deductions, Tax Reliefs and Rebates”

Select “Add New” > “Child/Parenthood Tax Rebate”

Fill in the relevant information and select “Yes” under “Claim one-time Parenthood Tax Rebate (PTR) to indicate share of PTR as agreed by you and your Spouse"

Select “Update"

If you have claimed this relief before, any unutilised balance will be carried forward automatically to offset future tax payable



For paper filing:

Complete the particulars of your child in Appendix 2 of Form B1 & B if this is your first time claiming this relief

If you have claimed this relief before, any unutilised balance will be carried forward without further action

Where can I find help?

View more details

IRAS hotline: 1800-356-8300

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Scheme last updated 19 Sep 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
